content.claim: Dormitory accommodation capacity is not currently a binding national constraint: dormitories can accommodate most applicants, with far more places than applicants and demand in decline, though this figure is aggregate across all secondary types and not disaggregated for 6-/8-year gimnazium applicants, who are younger (age 10-12) than typical 4-year-track boarders (age 14+).
content.context: Fresh live research through the School network planning lens
limitations: Aggregate data masks potential sector-specific shortfalls for early-track boarders
No separate data exists on kollégium welfare/supervision infrastructure calibrated for pre-adolescent (age 10-12) residents versus adolescents (age 14+)
Declining demand may reflect demographic contraction rather than genuine sufficiency
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
